cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out which of the options is a humorous edit of "new kids on the block." The original phrase is both a band name and a movie title. First, I should break down the original phrase: "New Kids on the Block." A humorous edit might involve a play on words or a pun. Let's look at each option. Option (A): "newgkids on the block" – This replaces "New Kids" with "newgkids," which doesn't seem like a typical pun. It might be a typo or a misspelling, but not particularly humorous as a deliberate edit. Option (B): "new kid on the block" – This is just a singular version of "New Kids on the Block." It's a common phrase in itself and doesn't seem like a humorous twist. It might be a variation but not a humorous edit. Option (C): "new kids on the clock" – This changes "block" to "clock." This could be a humorous play on words because "on the clock" is a common phrase meaning that someone is working or being paid for their time. So
